methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $46M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Cobod is a Series A–stage construction technology company in the Housing & Construction sector. The comparable universe consists of four private peers (ICON, Cuby Technologies, Inversion Space, Material Evolution), with no public companies identified in this narrow segment. The scarcity of public comps and same-stage private benchmarks limits traditional peer-median approaches.

A stage-progression valuation method is applicable here, using total capital raised to date ($46M) as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $92M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable data—only one peer exists in the Housing & Construction sector, falling short of the three-company minimum threshold necessary for statistical reliability. Consequently, the analysis relies on a single triangulation method.

The valuation analysis supports a range of $78M to $106M, centered on a $92M median. This range reflects inherent uncertainty around stage-progression multiples and the company's position relative to a thinly populated peer set. Key risks to valuation include construction technology adoption rates, capital intensity of scaling operations, and the absence of robust public market precedent for similar-stage companies in this subsector. Additional comps or forward guidance on unit economics would narrow this band.
